items.headline: Pakistan raises fuel prices up to 55% as Iran war chokes global oil supply
items.body: <cite index="51-1,56-5,56-6">Pakistan increased petrol by 43% and diesel by 55% as global oil prices surge amid the war disrupting the Strait of Hormuz</cite>. <cite index="56-7,57-15">The hike pushes a cash-strapped nation already battling high inflation into deeper crisis as crude oil prices in Dubai and Oman jumped 80 to 90%</cite>.

items.headline: Mediterranean migrant deaths near 1,000 in first three months of 2026
items.body: <cite index="61-2,62-4">At least 990 people have died crossing the Mediterranean since January, one of the deadliest starts to a year since 2014</cite>. <cite index="62-3,67-14,67-15">Central Mediterranean deaths rose by more than 460 from last year even as Italy recorded sharply fewer arrivals</cite>.

items.headline: Vietnam consolidates power as Communist Party chief assumes presidency
items.body: <cite index="71-3,71-4">Vietnam's National Assembly unanimously elected Tô Lâm as president, consolidating party and state roles in one figure</cite>. <cite index="75-4,77-5">The move breaks with Vietnam's tradition of shared leadership and mirrors power structures in China and Laos</cite>.

items.headline: United Nations reopens Khartoum headquarters after three years of war
items.body: <cite index="81-2,82-6">The UN reopened its headquarters in Sudan's capital for the first time since 2023, resuming operations as the war approaches its third year</cite>. <cite index="82-4,82-5,82-6">The conflict has displaced millions and left 33.7 million people requiring humanitarian assistance this year, up 3.3 million from 2025</cite>.
